figures.caption: Fig. 1. HYVE architecture. The preprocessor parses input strings with embedded structured segments into hybrid tabular views; the request-scoped datastore retains the full data; and the postprocessor inspects LLM outputs and, when needed, queries the datastore to enrich the response.

Fig. 2. Slot-filling tasks over 700 network telemetry samples. Top: baseline GPT-4.1 consumes ∼5000 tokens per request; HYVE reduces usage by 53%. Bottom: baseline GPT-4.1 fails on 30.6% of samples (scores ≤2); HYVE achieves almost perfect scores on all samples.

Fig. 3. Line chart over three years of USD exchange-rate data (778 points per series). Baseline GPT-4.1 truncates output arrays to 40–120 points, collapsing detailed time series into sparse straight-line segments. HYVE recovers all 778 points through data backfilling.
Fig. 6. Per-sample distributions on the Anom dataset (n=797). HYVE improves reasoning quality while reducing token consumption through the anomaly operator’s high-signal summarization.
TABLE II ABLATION STUDY OF HYVE WITH GPT-4.1
